content.content:
1→-- Create email schema tables for Gmail data storage
     2→
     3→-- Set schema context
     4→SET search_path TO :"EMAIL_SCHEMA";
     5→
     6→-- Gmail labels table
     7→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS gmail_labels (
     8→    label_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
     9→    label_name TEXT NOT NULL,
    10→    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
    11→);
    12→COMMENT ON TABLE gmail_labels IS 'Gmail label definitions';
    13→COMMENT ON COLUMN gmail_labels.label_id IS 'Gmail label identifier';
    14→COMMENT ON COLUMN gmail_labels.label_name IS 'Human-readable label name';
    15→
    16→-- Gmail messages table
    17→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS gmail_message (
    18→    gmail_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    19→    gmail_thread_id TEXT NOT NULL,
    20→    size_estimate INTEGER,
    21→    label_ids TEXT[],
    22→    subject TEXT,
    23→    sent_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
    24→    message_id_hdr TEXT UNIQUE,
    25→    from_addrs JSONB,
    26→    to_addrs JSONB,
    27→    reply_to_addrs JSONB,
    28→    headers JSONB,
    29→    body_html TEXT,
    30→    body_text TEXT,
    31→    body_text_as_html TEXT,
    32→    raw JSONB,
    33→    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
    34→);
    35→
    36→COMMENT ON TABLE gmail_message IS 'Gmail message data and metadata';
    37→COMMENT ON COLUMN gmail_message.gmail_id IS 'Unique Gmail message ID';
    38→COMMENT ON COLUMN gmail_message.gmail_thread_id IS 'Thread this message belongs to';
    39→COMMENT ON COLUMN gmail_message.size_estimate IS 'Estimated message size in bytes';
    40→COMMENT ON COLUMN gmail_message.label_ids IS 'Array of label IDs applied to message';
    41→COMMENT ON COLUMN gmail_message.subject IS 'Email subject line';
    42→COMMENT ON COLUMN gmail_message.sent_at IS 'When email was sent';
    43→COMMENT ON COLUMN gmail_message.message_id_hdr IS 'Message-ID header value';
    44→COMMENT ON COLUMN gmail_message.from_addrs IS 'Sender addresses structure';
    45→COMMENT ON COLUMN gmail_message.to_addrs IS 'Recipient addresses structure';
    46→COMMENT ON COLUMN gmail_message.reply_to_addrs IS 'Reply-to addresses';
    47→COMMENT ON COLUMN gmail_message.headers IS 'Full email headers';
    48→COMMENT ON COLUMN gmail_message.body_html IS 'HTML body content';
    49→COMMENT ON COLUMN gmail_message.body_text IS 'Plain text body';
    50→COMMENT ON COLUMN gmail_message.body_text_as_html IS 'Text body converted to HTML';
    51→COMMENT ON COLUMN gmail_message.raw IS 'Complete raw message data';
    52→
    53→-- Create indexes for performance
    54→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_gmail_message_thread_id ON gmail_message(gmail_thread_id);
    55→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_gmail_message_sent_at ON gmail_message(sent_at DESC);
    56→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_gmail_message_label_ids ON gmail_message USING GIN(label_ids);
    57→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_gmail_message_from_addrs ON gmail_message USING GIN(from_addrs);
    58→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_gmail_message_to_addrs ON gmail_message USING GIN(to_addrs);
    59→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_gmail_message_headers ON gmail_message USING GIN(headers);
    60→
    61→-- Email categorization tracking table
    62→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS email_categorization (
    63→    id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    64→    gmail_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES gmail_message(gmail_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    65→    category TEXT NOT NULL,
    66→    confidence TEXT CHECK (confidence IN ('high', 'medium', 'low')),
    67→    classification_method TEXT CHECK (classification_method IN ('rule', 'ai', 'manual')),
    68→    ai_model TEXT,
    69→    ai_prompt TEXT,
    70→    ai_response JSONB,
    71→    processed_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    72→    UNIQUE(gmail_id, category)
    73→);
    74→
    75→COMMENT ON TABLE email_categorization IS 'Email categorization results and metadata';
    76→COMMENT ON COLUMN email_categorization.gmail_id IS 'Reference to gmail_message';
    77→COMMENT ON COLUMN email_categorization.category IS 'Assigned category';
    78→COMMENT ON COLUMN email_categorization.confidence IS 'Classification confidence level';
    79→COMMENT ON COLUMN email_categorization.classification_method IS 'How the classification was determined';
    80→COMMENT ON COLUMN email_categorization.ai_model IS 'AI model used if applicable';
    81→COMMENT ON COLUMN email_categorization.ai_response IS 'Full AI response for debugging';
    82→
    83→-- Create index for categorization lookups
    84→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_email_categorization_gmail_id ON email_categorization(gmail_id);
    85→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_email_categorization_category ON email_categorization(category);
    86→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_email_categorization_processed_at ON email_categorization(processed_at DESC);
    87→
    88→-- Digest generation tracking
    89→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS digest_generation (
    90→    id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    91→    digest_type TEXT NOT NULL,
    92→    generated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    93→    email_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    94→    digest_content TEXT,
    95→    sent_to TEXT[],
    96→    status TEXT DEFAULT 'pending',
    97→    error_message TEXT
    98→);
    99→
   100→COMMENT ON TABLE digest_generation IS 'Track digest email generation';
   101→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_digest_generation_type ON digest_generation(digest_type);
   102→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_digest_generation_generated_at ON digest_generation(generated_at DESC);
   103→
   104→-- =====================================
   105→-- Email Classification Schema (from migration)
   106→-- =====================================
   107→
   108→-- Reference: Categories (Hierarchical)
   109→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS category (
   110→  category_id        SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
   111→  code               TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,              -- e.g. 'financial', 'financial.receipt', 'financial.statement'
   112→  display_name       TEXT NOT NULL,
   113→  description        TEXT,
   114→  category_level     TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (category_level IN ('top_level','subtype')),
   115→  parent_category_id INT REFERENCES category(category_id),  -- NULL for top-level
   116→  category_type      TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (category_type IN ('rule_based','ai_assisted','meta')),
   117→  gmail_label        TEXT,                              -- mapped Gmail label (e.g. receipts)
   118→  active             BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
   119→  created_at         TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
   120→  CONSTRAINT chk_parent_level CHECK (
   121→    (category_level = 'top_level' AND parent_category_id IS NULL) OR
   122→    (category_level = 'subtype' AND parent_category_id IS NOT NULL)
   123→  )
   124→);
   125→
   126→COMMENT ON TABLE category IS 'Hierarchical classification categories with top-level and subtype support.';
   127→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_category_parent ON category(parent_category_id) WHERE parent_category_id IS NOT NULL;
   128→
   129→-- Classification Session (One session per end-to-end pipeline attempt for a message)
   130→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS classification_session (
   131→  session_id         BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
   132→  gmail_id           TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES gmail_message(gmail_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   133→  pipeline_version   TEXT NOT NULL,                     -- semantic or git hash
   134→  started_at         TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
   135→  completed_at       TIMESTAMPTZ,
   136→  final_category_id  INT REFERENCES category(category_id),
   137→  final_confidence   NUMERIC(5,2),
   138→  final_rationale    TEXT,
   139→  final_keywords     TEXT[],                            -- aggregated salient terms
   140→  final_features     JSONB,                             -- consolidated feature map
   141→  ai_routing_used    BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,             -- Whether AI classification was invoked
   142→  status             TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'in_progress' CHECK (status IN ('in_progress','completed','aborted')),
   143→  triggered_by       TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'system',    -- system|human|reprocess
   144→  UNIQUE (gmail_id, pipeline_version, started_at)
   145→);
   146→
   147→COMMENT ON TABLE classification_session IS 'Top-level run context for staged classification of a message.';
   148→
   149→-- Classification Stage (Each discrete step in classification pipeline)
   150→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS classification_stage (
   151→  stage_id           BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
   152→  session_id         BIGINT NOT NULL REFERENCES classification_session(session_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   153→  stage_order        SMALLINT NOT NULL,                 -- sequence within session
   154→  stage_type         TEXT NOT NULL,                     -- e.g. 'rule_match','feature_extraction','ai_classification','human_review'
   155→  started_at         TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
   156→  ended_at           TIMESTAMPTZ,
   157→  decision_category_id INT REFERENCES category(category_id), -- if stage concluded with a primary category decision
   158→  decision_polarity  TEXT CHECK (decision_polarity IN ('positive','negative','none')),
   159→  decision_confidence NUMERIC(5,2),
   160→  weight_score       JSONB,                             -- weight score object: {total_weight, stage_type, categories_processed}
   161→  rationale          TEXT,                              -- summary rationale for primary decision
   162→  keywords           TEXT[],                            -- salient terms for this stage
   163→  features           JSONB,                             -- raw feature extraction output (for feature_extraction stage)
   164→  passed_to_next     BOOLEAN,                           -- true if pipeline continued
   165→  CONSTRAINT uq_stage_order UNIQUE (session_id, stage_order)
   166→);
   167→
   168→COMMENT ON TABLE classification_stage IS 'Discrete step in the classification pipeline.';
   169→
   170→-- Stage Category Evaluation (One row per (stage, category) considered)
   171→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS stage_category_eval (
   172→  eval_id            BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
   173→  stage_id           BIGINT NOT NULL REFERENCES classification_stage(stage_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   174→  category_id        INT NOT NULL REFERENCES category(category_id),
   175→  decision           TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (decision IN ('positive','negative','candidate','rejected')),
   176→  score              JSONB NOT NULL,                    -- score object: {score, confidence, scoring_components, rationale, selected_subtype_id, decision}
   177→  confidence         NUMERIC(5,2),                      -- probability or normalized
   178→  weight_components  JSONB,                             -- breakdown of rule contributions
   179→  scoring_components JSONB,                             -- Detailed scoring breakdown
   180→  selected_subtype_id INT REFERENCES category(category_id),  -- Selected subtype if category chosen
   181→  rationale          TEXT,                              -- per-category reasoning
   182→  keywords           TEXT[],                            -- per-category salient terms
   183→  feature_vector     JSONB,                             -- subset features relevant
   184→  created_at         TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
   185→  UNIQUE (stage_id, category_id)
   186→);
   187→
   188→COMMENT ON TABLE stage_category_eval IS 'Per-category evaluation details with comprehensive scoring breakdown.';
   189→
   190→-- Extracted Features (optional granular list)
   191→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS extracted_feature (
   192→  feature_id         BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
   193→  stage_id           BIGINT NOT NULL REFERENCES classification_stage(stage_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   194→  name               TEXT NOT NULL,                     -- e.g. 'has_tracking_number'
   195→  value              TEXT,                              -- normalized string form
   196→  importance         NUMERIC(5,2),                      -- relative weight/impact
   197→  evidence_snippet   TEXT,                              -- text snippet supporting feature
   198→  created_at         TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
   199→);
   200→
   201→COMMENT ON TABLE extracted_feature IS 'Atomic features derived at feature extraction stages.';
   202→
   203→-- Session Categories (Multi-Label Support)
   204→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS session_categories (
   205→  session_id         BIGINT NOT NULL REFERENCES classification_session(session_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   206→  category_id        INT NOT NULL REFERENCES category(category_id),
   207→  subtype_id         INT REFERENCES category(category_id),  -- Selected subtype within category
   208→  is_primary         BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
   209→  confidence         NUMERIC(5,2) NOT NULL,
   210→  category_score     NUMERIC(6,2),                      -- Raw category score from scoring engine
   211→  hard_assertion     BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,             -- Was hard assertion triggered
   212→  ai_classified      BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,             -- Was this from AI classification
   213→  scoring_trace      JSONB,                             -- Complete scoring trace for this category
   214→  label_order        SMALLINT,                          -- 1 for primary, 2 for secondary, etc.
   215→  created_at         TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
   216→  PRIMARY KEY (session_id, category_id)
   217→);
   218→
   219→COMMENT ON TABLE session_categories IS 'Multi-label classification results per session with scoring details.';
   220→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_session_categories_primary ON session_categories(session_id) WHERE is_primary = TRUE;
   221→
   222→-- Detected Attributes
   223→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS detected_attributes (
   224→  attribute_id       BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
   225→  session_id         BIGINT NOT NULL REFERENCES classification_session(session_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   226→  stage_id           BIGINT REFERENCES classification_stage(stage_id),
   227→  attribute_name     TEXT NOT NULL,                     -- e.g. 'self_sent', 'from_financial_institution'
   228→  attribute_value    TEXT,                              -- Value if not boolean
   229→  detection_method   TEXT,                              -- 'regex', 'domain_lookup', 'derived', etc.
   230→  detection_evidence TEXT,                              -- What triggered detection
   231→  influence_role     TEXT CHECK (influence_role IN ('CORE','PRIMARY_BOOST','SECONDARY_BOOST',
   232→                                                    'DISAMBIGUATOR','FILTER','STRUCTURAL',
   233→                                                    'DERIVED_LINK','NEUTRAL')),
   234→  categories_influenced TEXT[],                         -- Which categories this attribute affected
   235→  total_bonus_applied NUMERIC(4,2),                     -- Sum of all bonuses from this attribute
   236→  created_at         TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
   237→  UNIQUE (session_id, attribute_name)
   238→);
   239→
   240→COMMENT ON TABLE detected_attributes IS 'Attributes detected during classification and their scoring influence.';
   241→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_detected_attributes_session ON detected_attributes(session_id);
   242→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_detected_attributes_name ON detected_attributes(attribute_name);
   243→
   244→-- Domain Signal Resolutions
   245→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS domain_signal_resolutions (
   246→  resolution_id      BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
   247→  session_id         BIGINT NOT NULL REFERENCES classification_session(session_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   248→  domain             TEXT NOT NULL,
   249→  signal_type        TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (signal_type IN ('hard_assertion','strong_indicator','weak_signal')),
   250→  matched_category_id INT REFERENCES category(category_id),
   251→  confidence_level   TEXT,                              -- 'high', 'medium', 'low'
   252→  domain_type        TEXT,                              -- 'financial', 'newsletter', 'logistics', etc.
   253→  created_at         TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
   254→);
   255→
   256→COMMENT ON TABLE domain_signal_resolutions IS 'Domain-based signals resolved during classification.';
   257→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_domain_signals_session ON domain_signal_resolutions(session_id);
   258→
   259→-- Human Overrides / Corrections
   260→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS human_override (
   261→  override_id        BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
   262→  session_id         BIGINT NOT NULL REFERENCES classification_session(session_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   263→  previous_category_ids INT[],                          -- Previous category IDs (multi-label)
   264→  new_category_ids   INT[],                             -- New category IDs (multi-label)
   265→  previous_category_id INT REFERENCES category(category_id),  -- Deprecated, use arrays
   266→  new_category_id    INT REFERENCES category(category_id),     -- Deprecated, use arrays
   267→  reason             TEXT,
   268→  user_id            TEXT,                              -- operator identifier
   269→  created_at         TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
   270→);
   271→
   272→COMMENT ON TABLE human_override IS 'Manual corrections to classification decisions supporting multi-label.';
   273→
   274→-- Additional indexes for classification tables
   275→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_class_session_message ON classification_session(gmail_id, started_at DESC);
   276→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_stage_session_order ON classification_stage(session_id, stage_order);
   277→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_stage_decision_positive ON classification_stage(decision_category_id) WHERE decision_polarity = 'positive';
   278→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_eval_category ON stage_category_eval(category_id);
   279→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_eval_positive_negative ON stage_category_eval(category_id, decision) WHERE decision IN ('positive','negative');
   280→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_feature_name ON extracted_feature(name);
   281→
   282→-- Views for classification data
   283→CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW v_latest_decision AS
   284→SELECT DISTINCT ON (m.gmail_id)
   285→  m.gmail_id,
   286→  cs.session_id,
   287→  cs.final_category_id,
   288→  c.code AS final_category_code,
   289→  cs.final_confidence,
   290→  cs.final_rationale,
   291→  cs.ai_routing_used,
   292→  cs.completed_at
   293→FROM gmail_message m
   294→JOIN classification_session cs ON cs.gmail_id = m.gmail_id AND cs.status = 'completed'
   295→LEFT JOIN category c ON c.category_id = cs.final_category_id
   296→ORDER BY m.gmail_id, cs.completed_at DESC;
   297→
   298→COMMENT ON VIEW v_latest_decision IS 'Latest classification session per Gmail message (use v_latest_decision_labels for multi-label).';
   299→
   300→CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW v_latest_decision_labels AS
   301→SELECT
   302→  m.gmail_id,
   303→  cs.session_id,
   304→  cs.completed_at,
   305→  cs.ai_routing_used,
   306→  array_agg(
   307→    json_build_object(
   308→      'category_code', cat.code,
   309→      'category_name', cat.display_name,
   310→      'subtype_code', sub.code,
   311→      'subtype_name', sub.display_name,
   312→      'confidence', sc.confidence,
   313→      'score', sc.category_score,
   314→      'is_primary', sc.is_primary,
   315→      'hard_assertion', sc.hard_assertion,
   316→      'ai_classified', sc.ai_classified,
   317→      'label_order', sc.label_order
   318→    ) ORDER BY sc.label_order, sc.confidence DESC
   319→  ) AS labels
   320→FROM gmail_message m
   321→JOIN classification_session cs ON cs.gmail_id = m.gmail_id AND cs.status = 'completed'
   322→JOIN session_categories sc ON sc.session_id = cs.session_id
   323→JOIN category cat ON cat.category_id = sc.category_id
   324→LEFT JOIN category sub ON sub.category_id = sc.subtype_id
   325→WHERE cs.completed_at = (
   326→  SELECT MAX(completed_at)
   327→  FROM classification_session
   328→  WHERE gmail_id = m.gmail_id AND status = 'completed'
   329→)
   330→GROUP BY m.gmail_id, cs.session_id, cs.completed_at, cs.ai_routing_used;
   331→
   332→COMMENT ON VIEW v_latest_decision_labels IS 'Latest multi-label classification results with full details.';
   333→
   334→CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW v_category_hierarchy AS
   335→SELECT
   336→  parent.category_id AS top_level_id,
   337→  parent.code AS top_level_code,
   338→  parent.display_name AS top_level_name,
   339→  child.category_id AS subtype_id,
   340→  child.code AS subtype_code,
   341→  child.display_name AS subtype_name,
   342→  child.gmail_label
   343→FROM category parent
   344→LEFT JOIN category child ON child.parent_category_id = parent.category_id
   345→WHERE parent.category_level = 'top_level'
   346→ORDER BY parent.code, child.code;
   347→
   348→COMMENT ON VIEW v_category_hierarchy IS 'Hierarchical view of categories and their subtypes.';

-- Migration: Add parallel processing capabilities to email schema
-- Date: 2025-09-28
-- Purpose: Enhance existing schema for direct Node.js/TypeScript implementation with parallel processing support

-- Set schema context
SET search_path TO :"EMAIL_SCHEMA";

-- =====================================
-- 1. Enhance Gmail Message table for batch processing
-- =====================================

-- Add parallel processing metadata to gmail_message table
ALTER TABLE gmail_message
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS processing_batch TEXT,
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS processing_priority INTEGER DEFAULT 1,
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS processed_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS is_processed BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE;

COMMENT ON COLUMN gmail_message.processing_batch IS 'Batch identifier for parallel processing';
COMMENT ON COLUMN gmail_message.processing_priority IS 'Processing order within batch (1-100, higher = more urgent)';
COMMENT ON COLUMN gmail_message.processed_at IS 'When message processing completed';
COMMENT ON COLUMN gmail_message.is_processed IS 'Whether message has been fully processed';

-- Add indexes for batch processing optimization
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_email_processing_batch ON gmail_message(processing_batch, processing_priority);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_email_processed_date ON gmail_message(processed_at) WHERE is_processed = true;
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_email_unprocessed ON gmail_message(created_at) WHERE is_processed = false;

-- =====================================
-- 2. Enhance Classification Session for parallel processing metrics
-- =====================================

-- Add parallel processing metrics to classification_session
ALTER TABLE classification_session
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS processing_mode TEXT DEFAULT 'sequential' CHECK (processing_mode IN ('parallel', 'sequential')),
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS batch_size INTEGER DEFAULT 1,
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS performance_metrics JSONB,
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS config_version TEXT,
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS config_snapshot JSONB;

COMMENT ON COLUMN classification_session.processing_mode IS 'Whether parallel or sequential processing was used';
COMMENT ON COLUMN classification_session.batch_size IS 'Number of emails processed in this batch';
COMMENT ON COLUMN classification_session.performance_metrics IS 'Performance timing and parallelism data';
COMMENT ON COLUMN classification_session.config_version IS 'Hash of configuration used for reproducibility';
COMMENT ON COLUMN classification_session.config_snapshot IS 'Full configuration snapshot for debugging';

-- Performance metrics structure:
-- {
--   "totalDurationMs": 5430,
--   "avgEmailDurationMs": 543,
--   "parallelStagesCount": 4,
--   "databaseBatchOps": 12,
--   "stageTimings": {
--     "hard_assertions": 45,
--     "strong_indicators": 78,
--     "rule_scoring": 234,
--     "attribute_detection": 156
--   }
-- }

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_classification_session_performance ON classification_session(processing_mode, batch_size);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_classification_config_version ON classification_session(config_version);

-- =====================================
-- 3. Enhance Classification Stage for parallel execution tracking
-- =====================================

-- Add parallel processing context to classification_stage
ALTER TABLE classification_stage
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS execution_duration_ms INTEGER,
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS parallel_executed BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS dependency_stages TEXT[],
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS stage_configuration JSONB;

COMMENT ON COLUMN classification_stage.execution_duration_ms IS 'Stage execution time in milliseconds';
COMMENT ON COLUMN classification_stage.parallel_executed IS 'Whether this stage ran in parallel with others';
COMMENT ON COLUMN classification_stage.dependency_stages IS 'Other stages this stage depended on';
COMMENT ON COLUMN classification_stage.stage_configuration IS 'Configuration parameters used for this stage';

-- =====================================
-- 4. Enhance Stage Category Evaluation for parallel computation
-- =====================================

-- Add parallel computation tracking to stage_category_eval
ALTER TABLE stage_category_eval
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS computation_duration_ms INTEGER,
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS parallel_computed BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS cache_hits TEXT[],
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS parallel_components TEXT[];

COMMENT ON COLUMN stage_category_eval.computation_duration_ms IS 'Time spent computing this category evaluation';
COMMENT ON COLUMN stage_category_eval.parallel_computed IS 'Whether this evaluation was computed in parallel';
COMMENT ON COLUMN stage_category_eval.cache_hits IS 'Which calculations used cached results';
COMMENT ON COLUMN stage_category_eval.parallel_components IS 'Which parts of scoring were computed in parallel';

-- =====================================
-- 5. Enhance Detected Attributes for parallel detection
-- =====================================

-- Add parallel detection metadata to detected_attributes
ALTER TABLE detected_attributes
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS detection_duration_ms INTEGER,
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS parallel_detected BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS detection_dependencies TEXT[];

COMMENT ON COLUMN detected_attributes.detection_duration_ms IS 'Time spent detecting this attribute';
COMMENT ON COLUMN detected_attributes.parallel_detected IS 'Whether detection ran in parallel';
COMMENT ON COLUMN detected_attributes.detection_dependencies IS 'Other attributes this detection depended on';

-- =====================================
-- 6. Create new tables for enhanced digest generation
-- =====================================

-- Email Digest table for structured digest tracking
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS email_digest (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    generated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    digest_type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (digest_type IN ('daily', 'weekly', 'on_demand')),
    recipient_email TEXT NOT NULL,

    -- Content structure
    total_email_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    date_range_start TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL,
    date_range_end TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL,

    -- Template and delivery
    template_used TEXT NOT NULL,
    html_content TEXT,
    delivery_status TEXT DEFAULT 'pending' CHECK (delivery_status IN ('pending', 'sent', 'failed')),
    delivered_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
    delivery_error TEXT
);

COMMENT ON TABLE email_digest IS 'Structured email digest generation and delivery tracking';
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_email_digest_type ON email_digest(digest_type);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_email_digest_generated_at ON email_digest(generated_at DESC);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_email_digest_delivery_status ON email_digest(delivery_status);

-- Digest Categories table for category-specific digest sections
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS digest_category (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    digest_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES email_digest(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    category_name TEXT NOT NULL,
    email_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    priority INTEGER DEFAULT 1,

    -- Template data
    template_data JSONB,
    custom_summary TEXT
);

COMMENT ON TABLE digest_category IS 'Category sections within email digests';
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_digest_category_digest_id ON digest_category(digest_id);

-- Digest Emails table for tracking which emails are included in digests
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS digest_email (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    digest_category_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES digest_category(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    gmail_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES gmail_message(gmail_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,

    -- Email metadata for digest display
    subject TEXT NOT NULL,
    from_address TEXT NOT NULL,
    email_date TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL,
    snippet TEXT, -- First 150 chars of body

    -- Classification metadata
    confidence NUMERIC(5,2),
    categories TEXT[],
    urgency TEXT CHECK (urgency IN ('low', 'medium', 'high')),

    -- Digest-specific
    include_full_content BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    custom_highlights TEXT[]
);

COMMENT ON TABLE digest_email IS 'Individual emails included in digest categories';
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_digest_email_category_id ON digest_email(digest_category_id);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_digest_email_gmail_id ON digest_email(gmail_id);

-- =====================================
-- 7. Create indexes for performance optimization
-- =====================================

-- Parallel processing query optimization
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_classification_primary_category ON session_categories(category_id, created_at) WHERE is_primary = true;
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_stage_parallel_execution ON classification_stage(session_id, parallel_executed);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_attribute_parallel_detection ON detected_attributes(session_id, parallel_detected);

-- Configuration versioning for A/B testing
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_session_config_performance ON classification_session(config_version, processing_mode, completed_at);

-- =====================================
-- 8. Create views for parallel processing analysis
-- =====================================

-- View for parallel processing performance analysis
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW v_parallel_processing_performance AS
SELECT
    cs.session_id,
    cs.gmail_id,
    cs.processing_mode,
    cs.batch_size,
    cs.started_at,
    cs.completed_at,
    EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (cs.completed_at - cs.started_at)) * 1000 AS total_duration_ms,
    cs.performance_metrics,

    -- Stage parallelism summary
    COUNT(CASE WHEN cst.parallel_executed THEN 1 END) AS parallel_stages_count,
    COUNT(cst.stage_id) AS total_stages_count,

    -- Average stage duration
    AVG(cst.execution_duration_ms) AS avg_stage_duration_ms,
    MAX(cst.execution_duration_ms) AS max_stage_duration_ms,

    -- Attribute detection parallelism
    COUNT(CASE WHEN da.parallel_detected THEN 1 END) AS parallel_attributes_count,
    AVG(da.detection_duration_ms) AS avg_attribute_detection_ms

FROM classification_session cs
LEFT JOIN classification_stage cst ON cst.session_id = cs.session_id
LEFT JOIN detected_attributes da ON da.session_id = cs.session_id
WHERE cs.status = 'completed'
GROUP BY cs.session_id, cs.gmail_id, cs.processing_mode, cs.batch_size,
         cs.started_at, cs.completed_at, cs.performance_metrics;

COMMENT ON VIEW v_parallel_processing_performance IS 'Performance analysis for parallel vs sequential processing';

-- View for configuration version comparison
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW v_config_performance_comparison AS
SELECT
    config_version,
    processing_mode,
    COUNT(*) AS session_count,
    AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (completed_at - started_at)) * 1000) AS avg_duration_ms,
    MIN(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (completed_at - started_at)) * 1000) AS min_duration_ms,
    MAX(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (completed_at - started_at)) * 1000) AS max_duration_ms,
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (completed_at - started_at)) * 1000) AS median_duration_ms,
    AVG(batch_size) AS avg_batch_size
FROM classification_session
WHERE status = 'completed' AND completed_at IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY config_version, processing_mode
ORDER BY config_version DESC, processing_mode;

COMMENT ON VIEW v_config_performance_comparison IS 'Performance comparison across configuration versions and processing modes';

-- =====================================
-- 9. Migration validation
-- =====================================

-- Validate parallel processing enhancements
DO $$
BEGIN
    -- Check that new columns exist
    IF NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM information_schema.columns
        WHERE table_name = 'gmail_message'
        AND column_name = 'processing_batch'
        AND table_schema = current_setting('EMAIL_SCHEMA')
    ) THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Migration failed: processing_batch column not added to gmail_message';
    END IF;

    IF NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM information_schema.columns
        WHERE table_name = 'classification_session'
        AND column_name = 'processing_mode'
        AND table_schema = current_setting('EMAIL_SCHEMA')
    ) THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Migration failed: processing_mode column not added to classification_session';
    END IF;

    -- Check that new tables exist
    IF NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM information_schema.tables
        WHERE table_name = 'email_digest'
        AND table_schema = current_setting('EMAIL_SCHEMA')
    ) THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Migration failed: email_digest table not created';
    END IF;

    RAISE NOTICE 'Migration validation passed: All parallel processing enhancements applied successfully';
END
$$;

Perfect! Now I have enough context. Let me provide a comprehensive analysis of T042:

## Direct Answer to Your Question

**T042** is supposed to implement an **Email Persistence Layer with batch operations** in `packages/email-processor/src/persistence/email-repository.ts`. 

### What T042 Should Implement:

Based on the tasks.md, spec.md, plan.md, and database schema, T042 should create a repository pattern that handles:

1. **Batch Insert Operations** - Insert multiple Gmail messages into the `email.gmail_message` table efficiently
2. **Batch Update Operations** - Update processing metadata (processing_batch, processing_priority, processed_at, is_processed) for multiple emails
3. **Transaction Handling** - Ensure data integrity using the database transaction utilities from `db-utils.ts` (already implemented in T039)
4. **Data Integrity** - Handle constraints (unique gmail_id, references, etc.) and validate data before persistence
5. **Error Recovery** - Gracefully handle partial failures in batch operations with appropriate error context

### Key Database Fields T042 Must Support:

From the schema analysis (`03-email-schema.sql` and `add-parallel-processing.sql`):

**Core Gmail Message Fields:**
- `gmail_id` (PRIMARY KEY)
- `gmail_thread_id`
- `size_estimate`
- `label_ids` (TEXT[])
- `subject`, `sent_at`
- `message_id_hdr` (UNIQUE)
- `from_addrs`, `to_addrs`, `reply_to_addrs` (JSONB)
- `headers` (JSONB)
- `body_html`, `body_text`, `body_text_as_html`
- `raw` (JSONB)

**Batch Processing Metadata (from parallel processing migration):**
- `processing_batch` (batch identifier)
- `processing_priority` (1-100, higher = more urgent)
- `processed_at` (completion timestamp)
- `is_processed` (boolean flag)

### Repository Methods T042 Should Provide:

```typescript
interface EmailRepository {
  // Batch insert - insert multiple emails in a single transaction
  batchInsert(emails: EmailMessage[], options?: BatchOptions): Promise<BatchResult>
  
  // Batch update - update processing metadata for multiple emails
  batchUpdateMetadata(updates: EmailMetadataUpdate[], options?: BatchOptions): Promise<BatchResult>
  
  // Find unprocessed emails for batch processing
  findUnprocessed(criteria: QueryCriteria): Promise<EmailMessage[]>
  
  // Mark emails as processed
  markProcessed(gmailIds: string[], processingBatch: string): Promise<void>
  
  // Upsert - insert or update if exists (based on gmail_id)
  upsert(email: EmailMessage): Promise<void>
}
```

### Constitutional Alignment:

T042 directly supports:
- **Performance Through Parallelism** (Principle IV) - Batch operations enable concurrent database writes
- **Database Audit Trail Integrity** (Principle V) - Maintains all email data for classification explainability
- **Infrastructure Simplification** (Principle II) - Direct database operations replace n8n data routing

### Dependencies:

**T042 depends on:**
- ✅ T039 (Database connection pool with `db-utils.ts`) - COMPLETED
- ✅ T016 (EmailMessage type definition) - COMPLETED
- ✅ Database schema (gmail_message table with parallel processing columns) - COMPLETED

**T042 blocks:**
- T043 (Classification results persistence - needs email records to exist)
- T048 (Email query service - needs persisted data)
- T049 (Performance validation - needs batch operations)

### TDD Approach Required:

Per the task definition, T042 requires **TDD REQUIRED** with tests written FIRST in `packages/email-processor/tests/unit/email-repository.test.ts`:

**Test scenarios should cover:**
1. Batch insert with multiple valid emails
2. Batch insert with duplicate gmail_ids (constraint violation)
3. Batch update with processing metadata
4. Transaction rollback on partial failure
5. Upsert behavior (insert new, update existing)
6. Find unprocessed emails with various criteria
7. Mark emails as processed
8. Error recovery with partial batch failures

### Definition of Done:

(a) No TypeScript or lint warnings/errors  
(b) All tests passing (except those requiring future work)  
(c) Files created/updated have decent documentation  
(d) Code-reviewer agent validation completed

---

The proposed approach involves creating a simple query method in T042 that retrieves the last processed email timestamp, allowing subsequent ingestion cycles to fetch only new messages. This strategy ensures efficient, non-duplicative email collection across multiple synchronization attempts.

content.text: Now let me check the database schema for missing fields and run integration with the actual database to verify alignment:

content.content:
1→-- Create email schema tables for Gmail data storage
     2→
     3→-- Set schema context
     4→SET search_path TO :"EMAIL_SCHEMA";
     5→
     6→-- Gmail labels table
     7→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS gmail_labels (
     8→    label_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
     9→    label_name TEXT NOT NULL,
    10→    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
    11→);
    12→COMMENT ON TABLE gmail_labels IS 'Gmail label definitions';
    13→COMMENT ON COLUMN gmail_labels.label_id IS 'Gmail label identifier';
    14→COMMENT ON COLUMN gmail_labels.label_name IS 'Human-readable label name';
    15→
    16→-- Gmail messages table
    17→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS gmail_message (
    18→    gmail_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    19→    gmail_thread_id TEXT NOT NULL,
    20→    size_estimate INTEGER,
    21→    label_ids TEXT[],
    22→    subject TEXT,
    23→    sent_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
    24→    message_id_hdr TEXT UNIQUE,
    25→    from_addrs JSONB,
    26→    to_addrs JSONB,
    27→    reply_to_addrs JSONB,
    28→    headers JSONB,
    29→    body_html TEXT,
    30→    body_text TEXT,
    31→    body_text_as_html TEXT,
    32→    raw JSONB,
    33→    created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
    34→);
    35→
    36→COMMENT ON TABLE gmail_message IS 'Gmail message data and metadata';
    37→COMMENT ON COLUMN gmail_message.gmail_id IS 'Unique Gmail message ID';
    38→COMMENT ON COLUMN gmail_message.gmail_thread_id IS 'Thread this message belongs to';
    39→COMMENT ON COLUMN gmail_message.size_estimate IS 'Estimated message size in bytes';
    40→COMMENT ON COLUMN gmail_message.label_ids IS 'Array of label IDs applied to message';
    41→COMMENT ON COLUMN gmail_message.subject IS 'Email subject line';
    42→COMMENT ON COLUMN gmail_message.sent_at IS 'When email was sent';
    43→COMMENT ON COLUMN gmail_message.message_id_hdr IS 'Message-ID header value';
    44→COMMENT ON COLUMN gmail_message.from_addrs IS 'Sender addresses structure';
    45→COMMENT ON COLUMN gmail_message.to_addrs IS 'Recipient addresses structure';
    46→COMMENT ON COLUMN gmail_message.reply_to_addrs IS 'Reply-to addresses';
    47→COMMENT ON COLUMN gmail_message.headers IS 'Full email headers';
    48→COMMENT ON COLUMN gmail_message.body_html IS 'HTML body content';
    49→COMMENT ON COLUMN gmail_message.body_text IS 'Plain text body';
    50→COMMENT ON COLUMN gmail_message.body_text_as_html IS 'Text body converted to HTML';
    51→COMMENT ON COLUMN gmail_message.raw IS 'Complete raw message data';
    52→
    53→-- Create indexes for performance
    54→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_gmail_message_thread_id ON gmail_message(gmail_thread_id);
    55→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_gmail_message_sent_at ON gmail_message(sent_at DESC);
    56→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_gmail_message_label_ids ON gmail_message USING GIN(label_ids);
    57→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_gmail_message_from_addrs ON gmail_message USING GIN(from_addrs);
    58→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_gmail_message_to_addrs ON gmail_message USING GIN(to_addrs);
    59→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_gmail_message_headers ON gmail_message USING GIN(headers);
    60→
    61→-- Email categorization tracking table
    62→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS email_categorization (
    63→    id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    64→    gmail_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES gmail_message(gmail_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    65→    category TEXT NOT NULL,
    66→    confidence TEXT CHECK (confidence IN ('high', 'medium', 'low')),
    67→    classification_method TEXT CHECK (classification_method IN ('rule', 'ai', 'manual')),
    68→    ai_model TEXT,
    69→    ai_prompt TEXT,
    70→    ai_response JSONB,
    71→    processed_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    72→    UNIQUE(gmail_id, category)
    73→);
    74→
    75→COMMENT ON TABLE email_categorization IS 'Email categorization results and metadata';
    76→COMMENT ON COLUMN email_categorization.gmail_id IS 'Reference to gmail_message';
    77→COMMENT ON COLUMN email_categorization.category IS 'Assigned category';
    78→COMMENT ON COLUMN email_categorization.confidence IS 'Classification confidence level';
    79→COMMENT ON COLUMN email_categorization.classification_method IS 'How the classification was determined';
    80→COMMENT ON COLUMN email_categorization.ai_model IS 'AI model used if applicable';
    81→COMMENT ON COLUMN email_categorization.ai_response IS 'Full AI response for debugging';
    82→
    83→-- Create index for categorization lookups
    84→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_email_categorization_gmail_id ON email_categorization(gmail_id);
    85→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_email_categorization_category ON email_categorization(category);
    86→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_email_categorization_processed_at ON email_categorization(processed_at DESC);
    87→
    88→-- Digest generation tracking
    89→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS digest_generation (
    90→    id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    91→    digest_type TEXT NOT NULL,
    92→    generated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    93→    email_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    94→    digest_content TEXT,
    95→    sent_to TEXT[],
    96→    status TEXT DEFAULT 'pending',
    97→    error_message TEXT
    98→);
    99→
   100→COMMENT ON TABLE digest_generation IS 'Track digest email generation';
   101→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_digest_generation_type ON digest_generation(digest_type);
   102→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_digest_generation_generated_at ON digest_generation(generated_at DESC);
   103→
   104→-- =====================================
   105→-- Email Classification Schema (from migration)
   106→-- =====================================
   107→
   108→-- Reference: Categories (Hierarchical)
   109→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS category (
   110→  category_id        SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
   111→  code               TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,              -- e.g. 'financial', 'financial.receipt', 'financial.statement'
   112→  display_name       TEXT NOT NULL,
   113→  description        TEXT,
   114→  category_level     TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (category_level IN ('top_level','subtype')),
   115→  parent_category_id INT REFERENCES category(category_id),  -- NULL for top-level
   116→  category_type      TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (category_type IN ('rule_based','ai_assisted','meta')),
   117→  gmail_label        TEXT,                              -- mapped Gmail label (e.g. receipts)
   118→  active             BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
   119→  created_at         TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
   120→  CONSTRAINT chk_parent_level CHECK (
   121→    (category_level = 'top_level' AND parent_category_id IS NULL) OR
   122→    (category_level = 'subtype' AND parent_category_id IS NOT NULL)
   123→  )
   124→);
   125→
   126→COMMENT ON TABLE category IS 'Hierarchical classification categories with top-level and subtype support.';
   127→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_category_parent ON category(parent_category_id) WHERE parent_category_id IS NOT NULL;
   128→
   129→-- Classification Session (One session per end-to-end pipeline attempt for a message)
   130→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS classification_session (
   131→  session_id         BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
   132→  gmail_id           TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES gmail_message(gmail_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   133→  pipeline_version   TEXT NOT NULL,                     -- semantic or git hash
   134→  started_at         TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
   135→  completed_at       TIMESTAMPTZ,
   136→  final_category_id  INT REFERENCES category(category_id),
   137→  final_confidence   NUMERIC(5,2),
   138→  final_rationale    TEXT,
   139→  final_keywords     TEXT[],                            -- aggregated salient terms
   140→  final_features     JSONB,                             -- consolidated feature map
   141→  ai_routing_used    BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,             -- Whether AI classification was invoked
   142→  status             TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'in_progress' CHECK (status IN ('in_progress','completed','aborted')),
   143→  triggered_by       TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'system',    -- system|human|reprocess
   144→  UNIQUE (gmail_id, pipeline_version, started_at)
   145→);
   146→
   147→COMMENT ON TABLE classification_session IS 'Top-level run context for staged classification of a message.';
   148→
   149→-- Classification Stage (Each discrete step in classification pipeline)
   150→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS classification_stage (
   151→  stage_id           BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
   152→  session_id         BIGINT NOT NULL REFERENCES classification_session(session_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   153→  stage_order        SMALLINT NOT NULL,                 -- sequence within session
   154→  stage_type         TEXT NOT NULL,                     -- e.g. 'rule_match','feature_extraction','ai_classification','human_review'
   155→  started_at         TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
   156→  ended_at           TIMESTAMPTZ,
   157→  decision_category_id INT REFERENCES category(category_id), -- if stage concluded with a primary category decision
   158→  decision_polarity  TEXT CHECK (decision_polarity IN ('positive','negative','none')),
   159→  decision_confidence NUMERIC(5,2),
   160→  weight_score       JSONB,                             -- weight score object: {total_weight, stage_type, categories_processed}
   161→  rationale          TEXT,                              -- summary rationale for primary decision
   162→  keywords           TEXT[],                            -- salient terms for this stage
   163→  features           JSONB,                             -- raw feature extraction output (for feature_extraction stage)
   164→  passed_to_next     BOOLEAN,                           -- true if pipeline continued
   165→  CONSTRAINT uq_stage_order UNIQUE (session_id, stage_order)
   166→);
   167→
   168→COMMENT ON TABLE classification_stage IS 'Discrete step in the classification pipeline.';
   169→
   170→-- Stage Category Evaluation (One row per (stage, category) considered)
   171→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS stage_category_eval (
   172→  eval_id            BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
   173→  stage_id           BIGINT NOT NULL REFERENCES classification_stage(stage_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   174→  category_id        INT NOT NULL REFERENCES category(category_id),
   175→  decision           TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (decision IN ('positive','negative','candidate','rejected')),
   176→  score              JSONB NOT NULL,                    -- score object: {score, confidence, scoring_components, rationale, selected_subtype_id, decision}
   177→  confidence         NUMERIC(5,2),                      -- probability or normalized
   178→  weight_components  JSONB,                             -- breakdown of rule contributions
   179→  scoring_components JSONB,                             -- Detailed scoring breakdown
   180→  selected_subtype_id INT REFERENCES category(category_id),  -- Selected subtype if category chosen
   181→  rationale          TEXT,                              -- per-category reasoning
   182→  keywords           TEXT[],                            -- per-category salient terms
   183→  feature_vector     JSONB,                             -- subset features relevant
   184→  created_at         TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
   185→  UNIQUE (stage_id, category_id)
   186→);
   187→
   188→COMMENT ON TABLE stage_category_eval IS 'Per-category evaluation details with comprehensive scoring breakdown.';
   189→
   190→-- Extracted Features (optional granular list)
   191→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS extracted_feature (
   192→  feature_id         BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
   193→  stage_id           BIGINT NOT NULL REFERENCES classification_stage(stage_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   194→  name               TEXT NOT NULL,                     -- e.g. 'has_tracking_number'
   195→  value              TEXT,                              -- normalized string form
   196→  importance         NUMERIC(5,2),                      -- relative weight/impact
   197→  evidence_snippet   TEXT,                              -- text snippet supporting feature
   198→  created_at         TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
   199→);
   200→
   201→COMMENT ON TABLE extracted_feature IS 'Atomic features derived at feature extraction stages.';
   202→
   203→-- Session Categories (Multi-Label Support)
   204→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS session_categories (
   205→  session_id         BIGINT NOT NULL REFERENCES classification_session(session_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   206→  category_id        INT NOT NULL REFERENCES category(category_id),
   207→  subtype_id         INT REFERENCES category(category_id),  -- Selected subtype within category
   208→  is_primary         BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
   209→  confidence         NUMERIC(5,2) NOT NULL,
   210→  category_score     NUMERIC(6,2),                      -- Raw category score from scoring engine
   211→  hard_assertion     BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,             -- Was hard assertion triggered
   212→  ai_classified      BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,             -- Was this from AI classification
   213→  scoring_trace      JSONB,                             -- Complete scoring trace for this category
   214→  label_order        SMALLINT,                          -- 1 for primary, 2 for secondary, etc.
   215→  created_at         TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
   216→  PRIMARY KEY (session_id, category_id)
   217→);
   218→
   219→COMMENT ON TABLE session_categories IS 'Multi-label classification results per session with scoring details.';
   220→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_session_categories_primary ON session_categories(session_id) WHERE is_primary = TRUE;
   221→
   222→-- Detected Attributes
   223→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS detected_attributes (
   224→  attribute_id       BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
   225→  session_id         BIGINT NOT NULL REFERENCES classification_session(session_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   226→  stage_id           BIGINT REFERENCES classification_stage(stage_id),
   227→  attribute_name     TEXT NOT NULL,                     -- e.g. 'self_sent', 'from_financial_institution'
   228→  attribute_value    TEXT,                              -- Value if not boolean
   229→  detection_method   TEXT,                              -- 'regex', 'domain_lookup', 'derived', etc.
   230→  detection_evidence TEXT,                              -- What triggered detection
   231→  influence_role     TEXT CHECK (influence_role IN ('CORE','PRIMARY_BOOST','SECONDARY_BOOST',
   232→                                                    'DISAMBIGUATOR','FILTER','STRUCTURAL',
   233→                                                    'DERIVED_LINK','NEUTRAL')),
   234→  categories_influenced TEXT[],                         -- Which categories this attribute affected
   235→  total_bonus_applied NUMERIC(4,2),                     -- Sum of all bonuses from this attribute
   236→  created_at         TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
   237→  UNIQUE (session_id, attribute_name)
   238→);
   239→
   240→COMMENT ON TABLE detected_attributes IS 'Attributes detected during classification and their scoring influence.';
   241→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_detected_attributes_session ON detected_attributes(session_id);
   242→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_detected_attributes_name ON detected_attributes(attribute_name);
   243→
   244→-- Domain Signal Resolutions
   245→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS domain_signal_resolutions (
   246→  resolution_id      BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
   247→  session_id         BIGINT NOT NULL REFERENCES classification_session(session_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   248→  domain             TEXT NOT NULL,
   249→  signal_type        TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (signal_type IN ('hard_assertion','strong_indicator','weak_signal')),
   250→  matched_category_id INT REFERENCES category(category_id),
   251→  confidence_level   TEXT,                              -- 'high', 'medium', 'low'
   252→  domain_type        TEXT,                              -- 'financial', 'newsletter', 'logistics', etc.
   253→  created_at         TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
   254→);
   255→
   256→COMMENT ON TABLE domain_signal_resolutions IS 'Domain-based signals resolved during classification.';
   257→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_domain_signals_session ON domain_signal_resolutions(session_id);
   258→
   259→-- Human Overrides / Corrections
   260→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS human_override (
   261→  override_id        BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
   262→  session_id         BIGINT NOT NULL REFERENCES classification_session(session_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   263→  previous_category_ids INT[],                          -- Previous category IDs (multi-label)
   264→  new_category_ids   INT[],                             -- New category IDs (multi-label)
   265→  previous_category_id INT REFERENCES category(category_id),  -- Deprecated, use arrays
   266→  new_category_id    INT REFERENCES category(category_id),     -- Deprecated, use arrays
   267→  reason             TEXT,
   268→  user_id            TEXT,                              -- operator identifier
   269→  created_at         TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
   270→);
   271→
   272→COMMENT ON TABLE human_override IS 'Manual corrections to classification decisions supporting multi-label.';
   273→
   274→-- Additional indexes for classification tables
   275→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_class_session_message ON classification_session(gmail_id, started_at DESC);
   276→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_stage_session_order ON classification_stage(session_id, stage_order);
   277→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_stage_decision_positive ON classification_stage(decision_category_id) WHERE decision_polarity = 'positive';
   278→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_eval_category ON stage_category_eval(category_id);
   279→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_eval_positive_negative ON stage_category_eval(category_id, decision) WHERE decision IN ('positive','negative');
   280→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_feature_name ON extracted_feature(name);
   281→
   282→-- Views for classification data
   283→CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW v_latest_decision AS
   284→SELECT DISTINCT ON (m.gmail_id)
   285→  m.gmail_id,
   286→  cs.session_id,
   287→  cs.final_category_id,
   288→  c.code AS final_category_code,
   289→  cs.final_confidence,
   290→  cs.final_rationale,
   291→  cs.ai_routing_used,
   292→  cs.completed_at
   293→FROM gmail_message m
   294→JOIN classification_session cs ON cs.gmail_id = m.gmail_id AND cs.status = 'completed'
   295→LEFT JOIN category c ON c.category_id = cs.final_category_id
   296→ORDER BY m.gmail_id, cs.completed_at DESC;
   297→
   298→COMMENT ON VIEW v_latest_decision IS 'Latest classification session per Gmail message (use v_latest_decision_labels for multi-label).';
   299→
   300→CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW v_latest_decision_labels AS
   301→SELECT
   302→  m.gmail_id,
   303→  cs.session_id,
   304→  cs.completed_at,
   305→  cs.ai_routing_used,
   306→  array_agg(
   307→    json_build_object(
   308→      'category_code', cat.code,
   309→      'category_name', cat.display_name,
   310→      'subtype_code', sub.code,
   311→      'subtype_name', sub.display_name,
   312→      'confidence', sc.confidence,
   313→      'score', sc.category_score,
   314→      'is_primary', sc.is_primary,
   315→      'hard_assertion', sc.hard_assertion,
   316→      'ai_classified', sc.ai_classified,
   317→      'label_order', sc.label_order
   318→    ) ORDER BY sc.label_order, sc.confidence DESC
   319→  ) AS labels
   320→FROM gmail_message m
   321→JOIN classification_session cs ON cs.gmail_id = m.gmail_id AND cs.status = 'completed'
   322→JOIN session_categories sc ON sc.session_id = cs.session_id
   323→JOIN category cat ON cat.category_id = sc.category_id
   324→LEFT JOIN category sub ON sub.category_id = sc.subtype_id
   325→WHERE cs.completed_at = (
   326→  SELECT MAX(completed_at)
   327→  FROM classification_session
   328→  WHERE gmail_id = m.gmail_id AND status = 'completed'
   329→)
   330→GROUP BY m.gmail_id, cs.session_id, cs.completed_at, cs.ai_routing_used;
   331→
   332→COMMENT ON VIEW v_latest_decision_labels IS 'Latest multi-label classification results with full details.';
   333→
   334→CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW v_category_hierarchy AS
   335→SELECT
   336→  parent.category_id AS top_level_id,
   337→  parent.code AS top_level_code,
   338→  parent.display_name AS top_level_name,
   339→  child.category_id AS subtype_id,
   340→  child.code AS subtype_code,
   341→  child.display_name AS subtype_name,
   342→  child.gmail_label
   343→FROM category parent
   344→LEFT JOIN category child ON child.parent_category_id = parent.category_id
   345→WHERE parent.category_level = 'top_level'
   346→ORDER BY parent.code, child.code;
   347→
   348→COMMENT ON VIEW v_category_hierarchy IS 'Hierarchical view of categories and their subtypes.';

1→-- Migration: Add parallel processing capabilities to email schema

   109→
   110→-- =====================================
   111→-- 6. Create new tables for enhanced digest generation
   112→-- =====================================
   113→
   114→-- Email Digest table for structured digest tracking
   115→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS email_digest (
   116→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   117→    generated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
   118→    digest_type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (digest_type IN ('daily', 'weekly', 'on_demand')),

   121→    -- Content structure
   122→    total_email_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,

   127→    template_used TEXT NOT NULL,

   137→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_email_digest_delivery_status ON email_digest(delivery_status);
   138→
   139→-- Digest Categories table for category-specific digest sections
   140→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS digest_category (
   141→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   142→    digest_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES email_digest(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   143→    category_name TEXT NOT NULL,
   144→    email_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
   145→    priority INTEGER DEFAULT 1,
   146→
   147→    -- Template data
   148→    template_data JSONB,
   149→    custom_summary TEXT
   150→);
   151→
   152→COMMENT ON TABLE digest_category IS 'Category sections within email digests';
   153→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_digest_category_digest_id ON digest_category(digest_id);
   154→
   155→-- Digest Emails table for tracking which emails are included in digests
   156→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS digest_email (
   157→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   158→    digest_category_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES digest_category(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   159→    gmail_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES gmail_message(gmail_id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   160→
   161→    -- Email metadata for digest display
   162→    subject TEXT NOT NULL,
   163→    from_address TEXT NOT NULL,
   164→    email_date TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL,
   165→    snippet TEXT, -- First 150 chars of body
   166→
   167→    -- Classification metadata
   168→    confidence NUMERIC(5,2),
   169→    categories TEXT[],
   170→    urgency TEXT CHECK (urgency IN ('low', 'medium', 'high')),
   171→
   172→    -- Digest-specific
   173→    include_full_content BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
   174→    custom_highlights TEXT[]
   175→);
   176→
   177→COMMENT ON TABLE digest_email IS 'Individual emails included in digest categories';
   178→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_digest_email_category_id ON digest_email(digest_category_id);
   179→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_digest_email_gmail_id ON digest_email(gmail_id);
   180→
   181→-- =====================================
   182→-- 7. Create indexes for performance optimization
   183→-- =====================================
   184→
   185→-- Parallel processing query optimization
   186→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_classification_primary_category ON session_categories(category_id, created_at) WHERE is_primary = true;
   187→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_stage_parallel_execution ON classification_stage(session_id, parallel_executed);
   188→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_attribute_parallel_detection ON detected_attributes(session_id, parallel_detected);
   189→
   190→-- Configuration versioning for A/B testing
   191→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_session_config_performance ON classification_session(config_version, processing_mode, completed_at);
   192→
   193→-- =====================================
   194→-- 8. Create views for parallel processing analysis
   195→-- =====================================
   196→
   197→-- View for parallel processing performance analysis
   198→CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW v_parallel_processing_performance AS
   199→SELECT
   200→    cs.session_id,
   201→    cs.gmail_id,
   202→    cs.processing_mode,
   203→    cs.batch_size,
   204→    cs.started_at,
   205→    cs.completed_at,
   206→    EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (cs.completed_at - cs.started_at)) * 1000 AS total_duration_ms,
   207→    cs.performance_metrics,
   208→
   209→    -- Stage parallelism summary
   210→    COUNT(CASE WHEN cst.parallel_executed THEN 1 END) AS parallel_stages_count,
   211→    COUNT(cst.stage_id) AS total_stages_count,
   212→
   213→    -- Average stage duration
   214→    AVG(cst.execution_duration_ms) AS avg_stage_duration_ms,
   215→    MAX(cst.execution_duration_ms) AS max_stage_duration_ms,
   216→
   217→    -- Attribute detection parallelism
   218→    COUNT(CASE WHEN da.parallel_detected THEN 1 END) AS parallel_attributes_count,
   219→    AVG(da.detection_duration_ms) AS avg_attribute_detection_ms
   220→
   221→FROM classification_session cs
   222→LEFT JOIN classification_stage cst ON cst.session_id = cs.session_id
   223→LEFT JOIN detected_attributes da ON da.session_id = cs.session_id
   224→WHERE cs.status = 'completed'
   225→GROUP BY cs.session_id, cs.gmail_id, cs.processing_mode, cs.batch_size,
   226→         cs.started_at, cs.completed_at, cs.performance_metrics;
   227→
   228→COMMENT ON VIEW v_parallel_processing_performance IS 'Performance analysis for parallel vs sequential processing';
   229→
   230→-- View for configuration version comparison
   231→CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW v_config_performance_comparison AS
   232→SELECT
   233→    config_version,
   234→    processing_mode,
   235→    COUNT(*) AS session_count,
   236→    AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (completed_at - started_at)) * 1000) AS avg_duration_ms,
   237→    MIN(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (completed_at - started_at)) * 1000) AS min_duration_ms,
   238→    MAX(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (completed_at - started_at)) * 1000) AS max_duration_ms,
   239→    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (completed_at - started_at)) * 1000) AS median_duration_ms,
   240→    AVG(batch_size) AS avg_batch_size
   241→FROM classification_session
   242→WHERE status = 'completed' AND completed_at IS NOT NULL
   243→GROUP BY config_version, processing_mode
   244→ORDER BY config_version DESC, processing_mode;
   245→
   246→COMMENT ON VIEW v_config_performance_comparison IS 'Performance comparison across configuration versions and processing modes';
   247→
   248→-- =====================================
   249→-- 9. Migration validation
   250→-- =====================================
   251→
   252→-- Validate parallel processing enhancements
   253→DO $$
   254→BEGIN
   255→    -- Check that new columns exist
   256→    IF NOT EXISTS (
   257→        SELECT 1 FROM information_schema.columns
   258→        WHERE table_name = 'gmail_message'
   259→        AND column_name = 'processing_batch'
   260→        AND table_schema = current_setting('EMAIL_SCHEMA')
   261→    ) THEN
   262→        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Migration failed: processing_batch column not added to gmail_message';
   263→    END IF;
   264→
   265→    IF NOT EXISTS (
   266→        SELECT 1 FROM information_schema.columns
   267→        WHERE table_name = 'classification_session'
   268→        AND column_name = 'processing_mode'
   269→        AND table_schema = current_setting('EMAIL_SCHEMA')
   270→    ) THEN
   271→        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Migration failed: processing_mode column not added to classification_session';
   272→    END IF;
   273→
   274→    -- Check that new tables exist
   275→    IF NOT EXISTS (
   276→        SELECT 1 FROM information_schema.tables
   277→        WHERE table_name = 'email_digest'
   278→        AND table_schema = current_setting('EMAIL_SCHEMA')
   279→    ) THEN
   280→        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Migration failed: email_digest table not created';
   281→    END IF;
   282→
   283→    RAISE NOTICE 'Migration validation passed: All parallel processing enhancements applied successfully';
   284→END
   285→$$;
